name: Coto de las Canteras, Colegiata de Osuna y plaza de Toros — circular by the town of Osuna
name_original: Coto de las Canteras, Colegiata de Osuna y plaza de Toros — circular por la población de Osuna
region_intro: Hiking around Osuna offers diverse landscapes, including triangular hills, expansive olive groves, and striking mountain terrain. The region features well-maintained trails that traverse areas of significant botanical diversity and well-preserved fauna. Hikers can explore riparian forests along river courses and discover interesting geological formations carved by streams. The varied topography provides options for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Osuna The most popular hiking route is Coto de las Canteras, Colegiata de Osuna y plaza de Toros — circular by the town of Osuna, an easy 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This route offers views of significant local landmarks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñón de Algámitas Loop Trail, a difficult 3.4 miles (5.5 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain and provides panoramic views over Seville's olive groves. Local hikers also love the Laguna del Gosque Loop Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through the area around Laguna del Gosque, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Osuna is defined by triangular hills, extensive olive groves, and river-carved courses. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Osuna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 140 hikers have used komoot to explore Osuna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Osuna?
A: Osuna offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls through historical sites to more challenging ascents with significant elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Osuna?
A: The terrain around Osuna is quite varied, featuring triangular hills, expansive olive groves, and striking mountain landscapes, particularly in the Middle Subbetic Hills. Many trails also follow river-carved courses and pass through unique riparian forests.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Osuna?
A: Yes, Osuna has several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010628884/coto-de-las-canteras-colegiata-de-osuna-y-plaza-de-toros-circular-by-the-town-of-osuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto de las Canteras, Colegiata de Osuna y plaza de Toros — circular by the town of Osuna&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5.5 km trail offering views of local landmarks.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the Osuna area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009613445/pico-del-terril-and-penon-de-algamitas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Circular&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent choice. This difficult 19 km trail features substantial elevation gain and offers rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Osuna?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Osuna are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009634191/laguna-del-gosque-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Gosque Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 12.3 km option that explores the area around the lake.

Q: What kind of wildlife and natural features can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Osuna is rich in biodiversity. You might spot foxes, sparrow hawks, nightingales, and moorhens along river courses. The Endorheic area, particularly around the Gosque Lagoon, is a crucial stop for migratory birds like flamingos, geese, and herons. The region also boasts Mediterranean forests with aromatic herbs like thyme and rosemary.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Osuna's trails often blend natural beauty with historical interest. You can explore sites like the ancient Roman necropolis at 'Las Cuevas' via the Necropolis Path, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto de las Canteras&lt;/a&gt;, a historical quarry. The town itself features landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calle San Pedro Palaces&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4082732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;University of Osuna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Osuna?
A: The hiking trails in Osuna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from olive groves to mountain views, and the opportunity to discover both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Many trails provide stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009626125/penon-de-algamitas-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Algámitas Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers breathtaking vistas over Seville's expansive olive groves from its higher elevations.

Q: What are the geological features of the hiking areas around Osuna?
A: The region is characterized by interesting geological formations, particularly in the Middle Subbetic Hills. You'll encounter scarped landscapes and dramatic river-carved courses, where streams have sculpted the terrain over time, creating unique natural scenery.

Q: Are there any trails that feature lakes or significant water bodies?
A: Yes, the Endorheic area near Osuna is known for its beautiful shallow lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6037136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gosque Lagoon Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, and the Laguna del Gosque Loop Trail allows you to explore this unique aquatic environment, which is also a haven for migratory birds.
tips: These lands of ancient Urso, from where stone was extracted for constructions since before the Roman occupation, is also known as the Petra of Andalusia for its spectacular stone reliefs. Undoubtedly, this rocky substrate has been used as a stone quarry continuously at least since the Turdetan period, although it seems that its exploitation must have been intensified as a result of the important construction work carried out by the IV Count of Ureña in the 16th century, until the decade from the 60s of the last century that work on it is stopped.
Beautiful bullring, known for the performances of the bullfighters but currently the scene of one of the most epic scenes in the Game of Thrones series.

The Marques de la Gomera Palace stands out on this street. The architecture of this palace is interesting, a splendid work of the 18th century, carried out by Juan Antonio Blanco, whose façade was made between 1764 and 1765 and its size reminds us of the so-called colonial style.
The center of the old town is the Plaza Mayor with the town hall, the Art Nouveau casino (not a casino, but more of a club) and the Convento de la Concepción.

The Collegiate Church, the current main parish of Osuna, stands in the center of a promontory that dominates the town.
Two cloisters are carved on stone columns, whose segmental arches are supported by large abacuses decorated with grotesques.
